author: Peter Higgins + Claude (Anthropic) — Collective Build
purpose: Machine-readable learning ontology for the Higgins Unity Framework. Each concept is a learning unit (LU) with prerequisites, domain translations, document mappings, difficulty level, and estimated time. The dependency graph enables automated generation of individualized learning paths for any domain entrant.
design_principle: Loudspeaker-to-cortex: the machine assembles the signal path; the human receives structured knowledge matched to their cognitive entry point.

meta.result_breakdown: 42 numbered items across Areas A–F; 23 indexed formal results (21 theorems, 1 identification, 1 conjecture). 19 supporting definitions provide the formal scaffolding.

domain_translations._note: Rosetta layer. Every core HUF concept expressed in each domain's native vocabulary.

keff.acoustics: Effective driver count / crossover balance index

tv.formal: Total Variation TV(ρ, ρ') = ½Σ|ρᵢ − ρᵢ'|

mc4.formal: Fourth Monitoring Category — Ratio State Monitoring
mc4.mathematics: Degenerate state observer with zero estimation error

cl_pipeline.engineering: PLL-topology control architecture (topology and failure modes transfer; parameter values require calibration)

imc_pid.formal: Internal Model Control with single tuning parameter λ
imc_pid.mathematics: Operator inversion with filter on simplex tangent space

robustness_margin.mathematics: Reciprocal of the H∞ norm of T·Δₘ
robustness_margin.engineering: Safety factor on model uncertainty (μ=2.5 means ±50% parameter error tolerated)
robustness_margin.acoustics: Crossover filter robustness to driver parameter variation
robustness_margin.ecology: Monitoring resilience to ecological parameter uncertainty
robustness_margin.manufacturing: SPC robustness to process parameter drift

simplex.formal: Probability simplex Δⁿ⁻¹ = {ρ ∈ ℝⁿ : ρᵢ ≥ 0, Σρᵢ = 1}
simplex.engineering: Constraint surface: fractions sum to one

learning_units.name: Engineering Entry Point
learning_units.description: Why a process/control engineer should care about HUF. Origin in PLL control for BTL loudspeakers. The insight: governance of any constrained portfolio is structurally identical to industrial feedback control.

learning_units.name: Ecology Entry Point
learning_units.description: Why an ecologist should care about HUF. The Ramsar Article 3.2 problem: how to quantitatively monitor ecological character. HUF provides the mathematical method.

learning_units.name: Mathematics Entry Point
learning_units.description: Why a mathematician should care about HUF. A new operator on the probability simplex with connections to CoDa, information geometry, and lattice theory.

learning_units.name: Physics Entry Point
learning_units.description: Why a physicist should care about HUF. Partition functions, entropy, and the connection between statistical mechanics and portfolio governance.

learning_units.name: Acoustics Entry Point
learning_units.description: Why an acoustician should care about HUF. The BTL loudspeaker problem: PLL control for crossover networks. Where HUF was born.

learning_units.name: Governance Professional Entry Point
learning_units.description: Why a governance professional should care about HUF. The standard-of-standards concept. How mathematical rigour serves institutional monitoring.

learning_units.name: The Unity Constraint
learning_units.description: The fundamental constraint: Σρᵢ = 1. Why this single equation changes everything. Mass balance, conservation of proportion, the constraint surface.

learning_units.name: The Probability Simplex
learning_units.description: Δⁿ⁻¹ as the geometric space of all possible portfolios. Vertices, faces, interior. Why every constrained portfolio lives here.

learning_units.name: The Ratio-State Carrier (ρ)
learning_units.description: ρ as a point on the simplex. The carrier ontology: ρ carries the information, Keff is what you extract from it. FM radio analogy: ρ is the carrier wave, Keff is the demodulated audio.

learning_units.name: Scale Invariance
learning_units.description: Why doubling the total budget doesn't change ρ. Scale invariance means the ratio state is immune to changes in total magnitude — only the proportions matter.

learning_units.name: Unity Invariance and CL-01
learning_units.description: Result A.3: any corrected state must land back on the simplex. This is CL-01 — the validity gate. The normalization protocol that enforces the constraint.

learning_units.name: Total Variation Distance
learning_units.description: The house metric: TV(ρ, ρ') = ½Σ|ρᵢ − ρᵢ'|. Bounded in [0,1]. Measures how far a portfolio has drifted. The ruler on the simplex.

learning_units.name: CL-02: The Drift Trigger
learning_units.description: CL-02 uses TV as the phase detector. It answers: has meaningful drift occurred? Drift velocity vₜ = TV(ρₜ, ρₜ₋Δₜ)/Δt. Threshold triggers escalation.

learning_units.name: Shannon Entropy and Keff
learning_units.description: Keff(S) = exp(H(ρ)) where H = −Σρᵢ ln ρᵢ. The effective number of active components. Maximum at uniform distribution. The demodulated observable.

learning_units.name: Herfindahl Observable and Ordering
learning_units.description: Keff(H) = 1/Σρᵢ². Conservative complexity measure. Result B.4: Keff(H) ≤ Keff(S) always. Two lenses on the same carrier.

learning_units.name: CL-03: The Complexity Gauge
learning_units.description: CL-03 extracts Keff from ρ — the FM discriminator stage. Detects structural concentration even when TV is low. Complexity retention ratio χₜ = Keff,t / Keff*.

learning_units.name: The Four Monitoring Categories
learning_units.description: MC-1 (passive), MC-2 (mandated), MC-3 (question-driven), MC-4 (ratio state). How HUF's fourth category differs: self-referential, non-invasive, model-free, bidirectional, cross-cycle.

learning_units.name: MC-4 Deep Dive: Ratio State Monitoring
learning_units.description: The degenerate state observer. Silent drift vs. intentional reweighting. Mean Drift Gap. Quality Factor. Leverage. Orphan elements. The fuel gauge analogy. Death signal.

learning_units.name: Six Structurally Invisible Failure Modes
learning_units.description: Silent concentration. Orphan element growth. Quality-factor underweighting. OCC ratio drift. Dynamic portfolio gating instability. Cross-cycle drift accumulation. What MC-4 catches that MC-1/2/3 miss.

learning_units.name: CL-04: Verdict Logic
learning_units.description: Three-tier urgency classification: GREEN/YELLOW/RED. Gain-scheduled response based on TV and Keff readings. Hysteresis protocol prevents alarm chatter.

learning_units.name: Hysteresis and Alarm Management
learning_units.description: Escalation guard η, persistence count m. How hysteresis eliminates verdict jitter (Result D.6). Industrial alarm management mapped to governance.

learning_units.name: The Zero-Sum Tangent Space
learning_units.description: TₚΔ = {v : Σvᵢ = 0}. The linear space where corrections live. Any adjustment to ρ must have components that sum to zero — what goes up must come down. Result A.4.

learning_units.name: CL-05: Restorative Control
learning_units.description: PLL-style closed-loop correction. Bounded feedback on the tangent space. The Oppenheimer constraint: STORE until validated. Status: not yet deployed.

learning_units.name: PLL Structural Homomorphism
learning_units.description: Result D.3: The CL pipeline shares topological structure with a PLL — every PLL stage maps to a CL stage. Topology, failure modes, and tuning intuition transfer; parameter values and performance bounds require calibration in each domain.

learning_units.name: PLL Failure Mode Inheritance
learning_units.description: Cycle slipping, false lock, loss of lock, jitter accumulation — failure mode topology transfers from PLL to CL. Mitigations transfer as design intuition; quantitative bounds require domain-specific calibration. Result D.4.

learning_units.name: IMC-PID Tuning on the Simplex
learning_units.description: Single parameter λ controls the speed-robustness trade-off. FOPDT model identification via bump test. Result E.3: IMC-PID derivation via Padé approximation. House default λ ≥ θ guarantees stability.

learning_units.name: Robustness and Margin Analysis
learning_units.description: Result E.4: robustness to ±20% parameter mismatch. Result E.7: explicit robustness margin μ > 2.25 at λ = θ (>7 dB gain margin). Why the house default is safe.

learning_units.name: Simplex-Preserving Control
learning_units.description: Result E.5: The IMC-PID controller preserves the simplex constraint. Corrections stay on TₚΔ. The closed loop cannot violate Σρᵢ = 1.

learning_units.name: Reference Baselines and Staleness
learning_units.description: Three baseline types: declared (fixed), epoch (resetting), exponential (α-EMA tracking). Result E.9: reference staleness bound — how old can a baseline be before it becomes meaningless?

learning_units.name: SOPDT Extension
learning_units.description: Second-Order Plus Dead Time for cascade systems with multiple time constants. Result E.11: SOPDT IMC-PID gains. Derivative term τD for anticipatory correction.

learning_units.name: Supermodularity and Lattice Theory
learning_units.description: Results C.3, C.4: Monitoring domains form a lattice. Adding a domain increases value more when more domains exist (increasing differences). Topkis monotonicity applies.

learning_units.name: Greedy Bound and Knowledge Function
learning_units.description: Result C.6: The greedy (1−1/e) approximation bound. Result C.7: HUF knowledge function is supermodular. Why partial adoption still captures most of the value.

learning_units.name: Amplification Network Theory
learning_units.description: Result F.3: Theorem transport — proofs from one domain transfer to isomorphic domains. Result F.5: Self-training criterion. The weakest-link property. Complementarity.

learning_units.name: Multi-Phase Scaling Law (Conjecture)
learning_units.description: Conjecture F.4: K_total = Σaᵢ + β·[n(n−1)/2]·∏aᵢ. Additive individual terms plus multiplicative cross-links. Status: CONJECTURE — not yet proven.

learning_units.name: The Sufficiency Frontier
learning_units.description: 6,357,738:1 information reduction. Four levels of reduction. Fisher sufficiency. PKD theorem. How HUF departs from exponential family assumptions by exploiting the structural invariant Σρᵢ = 1.

learning_units.name: The HUF PreParser
learning_units.description: The O(N) algorithm: identify budget M, compute ρᵢ = mᵢ/M, verify Σρᵢ = 1, output ρ ∈ Δⁿ⁻¹. How any dataset becomes a point on the simplex.

learning_units.name: Boltzmann Precedent
learning_units.description: Statistical mechanics achieved 10²³:1 reduction via H(q,p) = const. HUF achieves 10⁶:1 via Σρᵢ = 1. Hamiltonian is physics-specific; unity constraint is algebra-generic.

learning_units.name: System Discriminator Intake (SD-01 to SD-04)
learning_units.description: Four-stage deterministic gate: SD-01 (Unity Guard), SD-02 (Temporal Sieve / JND threshold), SD-03 (Complexity Gauge), SD-04 (Gating Verdict). What enters the monitoring pipeline and what gets filtered.

learning_units.name: The Category Class Structure Tree
learning_units.description: The full taxonomy of HUF monitoring categories and classes. How MC-1 through MC-4 decompose into sub-classes. The classification system.

learning_units.name: The Triad Synthesis
learning_units.description: The three-way relationship: carrier (ρ), observable (Keff), metric (TV). How they interact. Why you need all three. The triad as the minimal complete monitoring apparatus.

learning_units.name: Case Study: Energy Portfolios
learning_units.description: HUF applied to UK, Croatia, and China energy generation mixes. ρ = generation proportions. Keff = energy transition index. Real data, real drift detection.

learning_units.name: Case Study: Backblaze Hard Drive Reliability
learning_units.description: HUF applied to hard drive manufacturer portfolios. ρ = manufacturer share of deployed drives. Data-rich: quarterly HDI data from Q1 2024 to Q4 2025.

learning_units.name: Case Study: Toronto Infrastructure (TTC)
learning_units.description: HUF applied to urban transit infrastructure. Downtime allocation, maintenance portfolio governance.

learning_units.name: Case Study: ESA Planck Satellite
learning_units.description: HUF applied to cosmological data. Changepoint detection that exactly matches the He-4 exhaustion event. The most abstract application — and the most dramatic validation.

learning_units.name: Case Study: Ramsar Wetland (Croatia)
learning_units.description: HUF applied to Ramsar wetland ecological character monitoring. The application that connects HUF to international environmental governance.

learning_units.name: The Acoustic Origin: Organic Digital Loudspeakers
learning_units.description: The full RWA paper. PLL control for BTL loudspeakers. DADC-DADI correction. Cortex-matched crossover tuning. Where HUF was born. The machine-to-cortex signal path.

learning_units.name: Where HUF Does Not Apply
learning_units.description: Honest about the limits. When the unity constraint does not hold. When TV is the wrong metric. When Keff is meaningless. Intellectual honesty as engineering practice.

learning_units.name: Evidentiary Tiers and Status Markers
learning_units.description: Three tiers: validated results, ADD* (requires formula addition), CONJECTURE (unproven). STORE status for CL-05. How HUF tracks its own uncertainty.

learning_units.name: The Hard Gate
learning_units.description: The validation criterion: real-data Keff on an actual portfolio. Currently unmet. No amount of formal proof substitutes for empirical validation.

learning_units.name: Implementation: Developer Manual
learning_units.description: How to build HUF in software. The Systems Developer Manual. From concept to code.

learning_units.name: Scrutiny Edition: Adversarial Review
learning_units.description: Every assumption challenged. Every weakness probed. The adversarial stress-test of the entire framework. Read last — you need to understand what it's attacking.

learning_units.name: Governance: The huf.org Model
learning_units.description: The tiered engagement model. How huf.org operates. Reference / Adoption / Stewardship. The Governance Council. How the standard evolves.

learning_units.name: Governance: Collective Review Provenance
learning_units.description: The 27 collective review trace documents. How 5 AI agents independently reviewed every formal result. The provenance chain.

learning_units.name: Governance: The Register Packet
learning_units.description: The machine-readable manifest of the entire framework. v3.1. Complete corpus inventory, status tracking, and dependency mapping.

learning_units.name: Controlled Subtraction Principle
learning_units.description: How four audience editions share one codebook. The Mathematician's Edition is the parent; each subsequent edition subtracts mathematical depth and adds domain framing. Same 42 numbered items (23 indexed formal results), different language.

learning_units.name: The HUF Contextual Language (This Ontology)
learning_units.description: Meta-learning unit. The structured language you are reading right now. How componentized concepts with prerequisites and domain translations enable automated, individualized learning paths. The fifth edition that generates all others.

learning_units.name: VCore Stack
learning_units.description: The Thunderstruck / Liquid Audio Engine / V∞Core computational stack. The software architecture beneath the framework.

learning_units.name: Tunable Sensing Architecture
learning_units.description: Adaptive sensing layer. How the monitoring pipeline adjusts its sensitivity based on portfolio characteristics.

learning_units.name: CL-05 Calibration Study
learning_units.description: Audit-grade calibration at the highest control layer. How CL-05 calibration ensures traceable, defensible governance responses.

learning_units.name: Dynamic Portfolio Gating
learning_units.description: Elements enter and leave the active portfolio. Gate function Gᵢ(t) ∈ {0,1}. Unity constraint maintained through recomputation. Instability risks.

learning_units.name: OCC 51/49 and the Death Signal
learning_units.description: Operator/tool ratio monitoring. When ρ_operator drifts toward 1.0 and ρ_tool toward 0.0 without decision record: deceptive drift. The death signal: once ρ_tool = 0, recovery is impossible.

learning_units.name: SOPDT Robustness Margin — The Quartic (Grok)
learning_units.description: Grok contribution: Explicit quartic polynomial for the critical frequency u* where |T·Δₘ| peaks under SOPDT. Coefficients k²r²u⁴ − 2k²r(4r+5)u³ + (−r²+5k²r+25k²)u² + (50k²+10r)u − (25r+25) = 0. μ(k,r₁,r₂) computed by substitution. Ramsar nutrient example: μ ≈ 2.48 at k=1, μ ≈ 3.12 at k=2.

learning_units.name: General nth-Order Robustness Margin (Grok)
learning_units.description: Grok contribution: Generalization to any plant order. IMC filter order n matches relative degree. Complementary sensitivity |T| = 1/[1+(ku)²]^(n/2). Critical-point equation yields degree-2n polynomial. Key property: μ > 2.2 for k ≥ 1 across all realistic parameter ranges. FOPDT (n=1) and SOPDT (n=2) recovered as special cases.
learning_units.evidentiary_status: PROVEN — algebraic generalization of SOPDT quartic

learning_units.name: FOPDT Tuning Workflow and Deployment Table (Grok)
learning_units.description: Grok contribution: Practical tuning table for FOPDT systems. k=1.0 (house default, μ≈2.55), k=1.5 (recommended pilot, μ≈3.00), k=2.0 (conservative, μ≈3.49). Five-step workflow: fit FOPDT → choose μ target → read k → compute gains → deploy in CL-05 supervised mode. Ramsar nutrient example with explicit Kc, τI, τD values.
learning_units.evidentiary_status: FRAMEWORK — tuning values proven, deployment workflow untested (hard gate not passed)
learning_units.caution: CL-03 remains ADD*. CL-05 remains STORE. Grok's claim of CL-03 empirical validation is premature — fitting historical data is necessary but not sufficient. Hard gate requires live MC-4 cycle on real portfolio.

path_generator._note: Algorithm for generating individualized learning paths from this ontology.
steps: 2. Select corresponding entry_point LU from meta.entry_points.
3. Topologically sort all LUs reachable from entry point via prerequisites.
4. For prerequisite disjunctions (A|B|C), select the LU matching entrant domain.
5. Filter optional specialist LUs based on declared interest/depth.
6. Sum estimated_minutes for total path time.
7. For each LU, resolve domain_translations to entrant's vocabulary.
8. Output: ordered list of {LU, document_paths, domain_translation, estimated_time}.
path_generator.minimum_viable_path: Entry point → LU-010 → LU-011 → LU-012 → LU-020 → LU-030 → LU-040 → LU-140 → LU-141 → one case study → LU-160

future_extensions.v1.1: Add assessment questions per LU for self-testing.
future_extensions.v1.2: Add prerequisite strength weights (hard vs. soft dependencies).
future_extensions.v1.3: Add domain-specific case study routing (auto-select based on entrant domain).
future_extensions.v2.0: Interactive path generator consuming this JSON. Input: domain + available hours. Output: personalized reading list with document links.
future_extensions.v3.0: Adaptive learning: track which LUs completed, adjust remaining path dynamically.
future_extensions.v4.0: Multi-modal: integrate audio/visual learning materials alongside documents.
